pattern: The adjacency matrix is an undirected graph whose connected components are counted by a flood fill from each unvisited city.
hints: Each city is a node and a 1 in the matrix is an undirected edge.
Counting provinces is counting connected components of that graph.
Iterate over cities; from each unvisited one run a DFS or BFS marking everything reachable, incrementing a counter once per launch.

stress.description: symmetric random adjacency matrix with sparse edges
